item.title: ‘The scrutiny is constant’: referee Anthony Taylor retires from football
item.summary: The 47-year-old took charge of five World Cup matches Taylor has refereed in the Premier League for 16 years Anthony Taylor has announced his retirement from elite refereeing after a distinguished career spanning more than two decades and 831 matches. One of England’s most accomplished officials, the 47-year-old officiated 668 matches in the professional game in England. He brings the curtain down on his career following his final appointment – the World Cup last-16 game between Portugal and Spain in the US. Continue reading...
